properties.name: Strait of Hormuz
properties.risk_note: Single-lane transit for most Gulf crude (Iraq, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait shares in this seed set); highest concentration risk for India's crude imports given Middle East import share.

properties.name: Bab el-Mandeb
properties.risk_note: Southern gateway to the Red Sea/Suez corridor; exposed to Yemen-linked maritime security incidents that have historically forced Cape of Good Hope rerouting.

properties.name: Suez Canal
properties.risk_note: Narrow-canal transit connecting Mediterranean/Black Sea origins to the Red Sea; a single grounding or closure event can back up global tanker queues for weeks (illustrative modelling assumption).

properties.name: Cape of Good Hope (route waypoint)
properties.risk_note: Not a strait but the standard reroute waypoint when Suez/Bab el-Mandeb is avoided; adds roughly 1-2 weeks of transit time for Atlantic-basin and West African crude to India (illustrative).

properties.name: Strait of Malacca
properties.risk_note: Primary Pacific-to-Indian-Ocean transit for cargoes routed via the Panama Canal/Pacific rather than around Africa; congestion and piracy risk are the main modelled hazards.
